sentence_reviews.text: Behavior adapts from context, demonstrations, feedback, or computation at inference time without conventional retraining.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Snell et al. 2024 arXiv abstract: LLMs improve outputs using more test-time/inference-time computation; adaptive distribution updates given prompt at test time; AIMA contents: learning and NLP chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inference-time computation and prompt-conditioned adaptation are directly supported. Demonstrations/context are not directly established by the inspected test-time source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of In-context & test-time learning is to connect Self-supervised learning, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Continual learning & test-time adaptation pipelines, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Snell et al. 2024 abstract: test-time compute, inference methods, search against verifier reward models, adaptive response distribution; AIMA contents: learning and NLP chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: Test-time adaptation and reasoning-compute links are partially supported, but self-supervised learning, sequences, continual pipelines, and CoT are not directly supported as a full role statement.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Continual learning & test-time adaptation pipelines
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Snell et al. 2024 abstract: updating model distribution over a response adaptively given the prompt at test time.
sentence_reviews.notes: The test-time adaptation part is supported; continual learning pipelines are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Snell et al. 2024 abstract: scaling inference-time compute and search against process-based verifier reward models for challenging prompts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reasoning-time compute is supported broadly, but chain-of-thought and latent reasoning are not named in inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Self-supervised learning, Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: learning and NLP chapters; Snell et al. 2024 abstract discusses prompt difficulty and test-time strategy variation, not this general limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The exact limitation and dependency list are not supported by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: In-context & test-time learning follows the learning setup expressed by Self-supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: learning and NLP chapters; Snell et al. 2024 abstract: LLM test-time compute, no explicit self-supervised pretraining setup.
sentence_reviews.notes: Modern LLM in-context behavior often relies on self-supervised pretraining, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish this edge.

sentence_reviews.text: In-context & test-time learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Snell et al. 2024 abstract: prompts, responses, LLM inference-time compute; AIMA contents: NLP chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: LLM prompts/responses imply sequences, but the inspected evidence does not directly discuss sequences/time-series as an ingredient.

sentence_reviews.text: In-context & test-time learning uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: deep learning and NLP chapters; PML listed for ML background but not directly inspected for this claim.
sentence_reviews.notes: Embeddings are standard in LLMs, but the cited/inspected evidence is too broad for direct verification.

sentence_reviews.text: In-context & test-time learning follows the learning setup expressed by Meta-learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents: learning chapters; Snell et al. 2024 abstract: test-time compute, no meta-learning framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not support meta-learning as the training setup.

edge_reviews.notes: Plausible for LLMs but not directly supported by inspected sources as a common trained_under edge.
Prompt/response operation suggests sequence processing; core strength is plausible but not directly evidenced.
Embeddings are common in transformer/LLM implementations, but inspected evidence is too broad.
No inspected source supports meta-learning as a common trained_under dependency for in-context/test-time learning.
